title: Source Route Inventory Standard
purpose: Defines the deterministic route inventory agents must create before runtime UX observation so every route, dynamic pattern, layout chain, and exclusion is visible.

include: directories that compile to UI routes through page files
not-found and error-adjacent UI when user-visible
excludeFromCompiledRouteRows: shared/internal folders that do not compile as app routes
recordAsExclusions: route-like folders skipped because they do not compile
API routes excluded from UX route audit scope

rules: The route inventory is the first audit control surface.
Dynamic and catch-all patterns must be represented even when concrete runtime values are unknown.
Route groups must be preserved as metadata but must not add URL segments.
Underscore folders are intentionally excluded from compiled route UX scope and must have exclusion records.
The existing app-route-slug generator may be used as reference only; it is not the audit source of truth if it omits dynamic routes.
The inventory must be stable and sorted so future diffs show meaningful route changes.

Source/runtime behavior discovered during inventory must be routed to the relevant App Behavior row, shared spec, behavior thread/parent container, runtime drift row, pending decision, ideal/future record, or explicit bounded gap.
The route inventory is not final behavior authority. It may carry evidence, but App Behavior remains the durable destination for behavior truth.
